titleStmt.title: Letter from William Haldane Porter to Edward O'Farrell, 1 January 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Haldane Porter

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from William Haldane Porter (1867-1944) to Edward O'Farrell (c.1857-1926). Haldane's letter accompanies an official correspondence relating to the cases of three Alsatians who volunteered to join the French Army.William Haldane Porter served as His Majesty's Inspector of Aliens for the Home Office for over 25 years. Popular with both his seniors and also his staff, he was known affectionately to one and all as ''The Chief.'' Porter introduced some of the first legislation since Napoleonic times to attempt to control alien entry and departure to and from the United Kingdom. He was knighted for his work in 1917. Sir Edward O'Farrell served as the Assistant Under Secretary to the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland, Sir Ivor Guest Churchill, Lord Wimborne.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
HOME OFFICE. 1st January, 1916.
18027 Dear Sir E. O'Farrell, With reference to your minute of 9th Dec- ember on our file respecting Mrs. Josephine Bath (your reference 20026 -29/11/15) I enclose copy of a letter which I have received from Willmann. Yours faithfully, W. Haldane Porter Under Secretary
Submitted. Those papers deal with
the cases of 3 Alsatians who volunteered to
join the French Army. It would be an advantage
to have an official intention from the French
Consul of the result of their applications
WM
3/1
Londonderry, December, 20th 1915 R.WILLMANN Coiffeur and Artistic Hair Worker Dear Sir, In reply to your letter of the 15th December
I beg to say that I was exempted from military service
in the French Army, so it will not be necessary for my
sister to come to Ireland. Yours faithfully, (Sgd) Romain Willmann.
